cards.text: Pass by address to const is declared as {{c1::void func(const int* ptr)}}. This prevents modifying the value but allows {{c2::nullptr}}.

cards.front: When should you use pass by const pointer vs pass by const reference?
cards.back: Use pass by const pointer when nullptr is a meaningful value (optional parameter). Use pass by const reference when the parameter must always be valid.

cards.question: What's wrong with this code?
cards.answer: Cannot modify value through pointer to const. The const applies to the pointed-to value.
cards.explanation: Pointer to const prevents modification of the value, providing read-only access.

cards.concept: Choosing between pass by address and pass by reference
cards.explanation:
Decision guide:
  • Pass by reference: parameter must be valid (preferred)
  • Pass by address: nullptr is meaningful (optional param)
  • Add const if not modifying
  • For fundamentals, use pass by value
examples: void must(int& x);           // Must be valid
void optional(int* x);       // Can be nullptr
void readOnly(const int& x); // Can't modify, must be valid
void optRead(const int* x);  // Can't modify, can be nullptr

cards.text: Prefer {{c1::pass by reference}} over pass by address unless {{c2::nullptr}} is a meaningful value for the parameter.
Pass by {{c1::const}} pointer (const T*) provides {{c2::read-only}} access while allowing {{c3::nullptr}} as a valid value.
